topic: Why Gyms Want you to never show up

packaging.title: Why Gyms Are Built to Make You Quit
altTitles: The Gym Business Model Needs You Absent
Why Gyms Sell More Memberships Than They Fit
packaging.description: A breakdown of the gym membership business model, why fitness clubs oversell memberships far beyond capacity, and how no-shows quietly fund the entire industry.

thumbnail.visualIdea: An empty gym floor with rows of unused treadmills, a single dusty membership card in the foreground

packaging.hookPromise: The average gym sells memberships to ten times more people than its building could ever hold on one day — and the whole business quietly depends on you forgetting to come.
openLoops: What happens to a gym's profit if every member actually showed up on the same morning?
Why does the contract make canceling harder than signing up?
Why are the cheapest memberships the most profitable ones for the gym?
packaging.payoff: Big-box gyms intentionally sell roughly 10x more memberships than capacity, and roughly half of members never go — meaning the gym's entire margin is built on ghost members paying monthly for a service they don't use, by design.
packaging.whyItWorks: Nearly everyone has signed a gym membership and quit, so the betrayal angle hits a universal personal experience while exposing a hidden industry math nobody told them.
